title: Rituals
subTitle: There are ceremonies around the world that induce awe and agony as they mark the events of a person's life through birth, coming of age, marriage and death.

title: Wonders of the Sun with Dara Ó Briain
subTitle: Dara looks into the formation of the Sun and heads to Texas to witness a solar eclipse.

title: Baby Chimp Rescue
subTitle: The filmmaker visits Jimmy and Jenny, who rescue a chained orphan chimpanzee named Miracle despite already caring for twenty one baby chimps.

title: Mammals
subTitle: Water challenges air-breathing mammals, with only a small fraction truly adapted to marine life, like whales and dolphins that showcase remarkable intelligence and social behaviours.

title: North America with Simon Reeve
subTitle: Adventurer Simon Reeve journeys across North America, revealing glaciers, deserts, jungles, immense wealth, stark poverty, political divides, and human resilience amid dramatic landscapes.

title: Baby Chimp Rescue

title: Eva Longoria: Searching for Mexico
subTitle: Travelling to the mountains of Southern Mexico, Eva Longoria discovers the hidden magic of Oaxaca that boasts some of the finest ingredients in Mexico, including chocolate, corn, tomatoes.

title: Mammals
subTitle: Mammals have evolved to master cold environments, showcasing unique adaptations like thick fur and hibernation. This filmmaker sheds light on the animals that thrive in these harsh conditions.

title: Rituals
subTitle: The host explores various rituals from all over the world, shedding light on their cultural roots, historical background and emotional significance.

title: Wonders of the Sun with Dara Ó Briain
subTitle: Dara explores the Sun's darker side, starting in Houston with ex-NASA astronaut Terry Virts, who reveals its health risks. At NASA's Johnson Space Center, Dara learns how scientists track solar flares and the South Atlantic Anomaly. He examines.

title: Exploring India with Bettany Hughes
subTitle: Bettany Hughes journeys throughout India, starting with Varanasi, one of the country's holiest cities. Then, she explores Fatehpur Sikri, the Imperial capital of the Mughal Emperor Akbar.

title: Japan with Sue Perkins
subTitle: Sue Perkins begins her journey at the capital of Japan, Tokyo, where she trains with a female sumo wrestling team, meets a family who live with robots, and attends a solo wedding.

title: Joanna Lumley's Hidden Caribbean: Havana to Haiti
subTitle: Joanna Lumley starts her journey in Havana in a boxing gym, where she meets one of Cuba's rising stars.

title: The Secret Genius of Modern Life
subTitle: Hanna uncovers the wild origins of the modern-day rollercoaster and gets the inside story on the UK's newest, tallest, and fastest coaster called the Thorpe Park's Hyperia.

title: Eva Longoria: Searching for Mexico
subTitle: Mexico City is one of the world's emerging food destinations and it's also Eva Longoria's second home. She showcases some of her favourite spots amid the city's ever-changing culinary scene.

title: The Ganges With Sue Perkins
subTitle: Sue Perkins begins her journey in the highest mountains on earth, the Himalayas, in order to trace the course of the Ganges and tell the story of modern India.

title: Exploring India with Bettany Hughes
subTitle: Bettany visits Thanjavur and enjoys a splendid ride through the hills of Tamil Nadu aboard the Nilgiri Mountain Railway. She then meets the Toda people, who still revere the advent of the train.

title: Japan with Sue Perkins
subTitle: Sue Perkins immerses herself in Japanese culture, enjoying a night with geishas and joining ama divers for a free diving experience in the ocean.

title: Joanna Lumley's Hidden Caribbean: Havana to Haiti
subTitle: Joanna visits Guantánamo before heading to Haiti, the land of Voodoo.

subTitle: The presenter reveals the incredible tales behind the development of the contemporary world's marvellous technology and unearths their mysteries.

title: Eva Longoria: Searching for Mexico
subTitle: In the jungles of Yucatan State, on the eastern edge of Mexico, Longoria explores the home of the ancient Maya, where a fierce pride in that ancestry still permeates every custom and dish, even in the most innovative kitchens.

title: The Ganges with Sue Perkins
subTitle: Comedian Sue Perkins follows the course of the sacred Ganges river in India and meets the people who live, work and worship along its banks.

subTitle: Steve Backshall seeks deadly animals in the capital of Suriname. Additionally, he observes snakes in people's gardens and snail-catching kites.

title: A Perfect Planet
subTitle: Without volcanoes, there would be no life on Earth. Although destructive, magma from the planet's molten core forms land and mineral-rich ash from eruptions fertilises the surface.

title: China: Nature's Ancient Kingdom
subTitle: Tibetan Qinghai Plateau is home to animals such as snow leopards and foxes who have adapted to survive in the rugged landscape of western China.

title: Blue Planet II
subTitle: The filmmaker gives a retrospective insight into the behavioural patterns and natural habitats of several rare but strange creatures living deep under the sea.

title: Planet Earth II
subTitle: Remote islands offer sanctuary for some of the planet's strangest and rarest creatures.

title: Frozen Planet II
subTitle: David Attenborough narrates an icy adventure through the world's frozen zones, where wolves hunt, penguins huddle, and life thrives against the most extreme odds.

title: Wild Scandinavia
subTitle: While the coasts of Scandinavia are wild and unpredictable, it is also home to a wide rage of fauna like sea eagles, otters, base jumpers and orca.

title: Planet Earth III
subTitle: David Attenborough potrays the efforts of numerous conservation advocates who risk everything in order to rescue wildlife.

title: Big Little Journeys
subTitle: The filmmaker captures the journeys of two young creatures as one navigates towering forests in Canada in search of wetlands while the other follows distant lights in South Africa.

title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
subTitle: David Attenborough explores the continent of Antarctica and the challenges faced by its inhabitants due to changing climate.

title: Wild Alaska
subTitle: During springtime in Alaska, wilderness awakens and chaos ensues among various animals as they compete with each other for resources.

title: A Perfect Planet
subTitle: The host discusses various methods in which Earth and its natural resources like volcanoes, water bodies and other environmental phenomenons mould themselves in order to support life.

title: Nature's Greatest Dancers
subTitle: Steve Backshall explores the various animals, like ring-tailed lemurs and a tiny brown banana frog, that use amorous glances and tantalising moves in pursuit of romance.

title: The Hunt
subTitle: The contests between predators and prey are the most dramatic events in nature. Explore the extraordinary range of techniques employed by animals in order to trap their target.

title: Dynasties
subTitle: In Senegal, West Africa, live a group of chimpanzees, led by an alpha male named David. He has already been alpha for 3 years - a time when leaders here are usually overthrown. To make matters worse, David has no allies - no-one to help him.

title: Serengeti II
subTitle: Serengeti II

title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
subTitle: The presenter explores Australia, a land cast adrift at the time of the dinosaurs. Additionally, the weird and wonderful animals marooned in this place exist like nowhere else on Earth.

subTitle: The filmmaker sheds light on the lives of individual African big cats and explores their behaviour that allows them to thrive in the challenging and breathtaking landscapes of Botswana.

title: Joanna Lumley's Spice Trail Adventure
subTitle: The last leg of Joanna Lumley's journey takes her to Zanzibar, the spice island of Africa where merchants from around the world have left their mark.

title: Inside the Factory
subTitle: Gregg Wallace follows the production of pocket-sprung mattresses, from the arrival of hard steel to soft bedding heading out of dispatch, at a massive factory.

title: Chasing Monsters
subTitle: Cyril Chauquet is assigned the dangerous task of capturing an electric eel that is believed to emit a shock powerful enough to kill a human.
